metadata.name: Validate Workflow
metadata.description: Validates a workflow YAML against its declared validation schema and checks structural integrity.

task.instructions:
You are validating a workflow YAML file for structural correctness and completeness.
## Step 1: Identify the workflow file
If a workflow path was passed via the validate-workflow attribute, load that file. If no path was provided, ask the user to specify the workflow YAML to validate.
## Step 2: Read and parse the workflow
Load the workflow YAML. Extract these fields: - name (required) - description (required) - config_source (required — path to config.yaml) - instructions OR steps_path (at least one required) - validation (optional — path to a validation schema/checklist)
## Step 3: Load validation schema (if declared)
Check the workflow YAML for a `validation` property. If present: - Load the file at that path - Use it as the checklist for detailed validation in Step 4
If no validation property is declared, use only the structural checks in Step 4.
## Step 4: Validate
### Structural checks (always run): - [ ] `name` field exists and is non-empty - [ ] `description` field exists and is non-empty - [ ] `config_source` field exists and points to a file that exists - [ ] Either `instructions` (inline) or `steps_path` (directory) is defined - [ ] If `steps_path` is defined, the directory exists and contains at least one step file - [ ] If `validation` is defined, the referenced file exists - [ ] All other file paths referenced in the YAML resolve to existing files
### Schema checks (only if validation schema was loaded): - Run each item in the validation checklist against the workflow - Mark each as PASS or FAIL with a brief reason
## Step 5: Report
Output a validation report in this format:
``` WORKFLOW VALIDATION REPORT ══════════════════════════ File: {path to workflow} Name: {workflow name}
Structural Checks: ✓ name: present ✓ description: present ✗ steps_path: directory not found at {path} ...
Schema Checks: {N/A if no schema, or list results} ✓ {check description} ✗ {check description} — {reason}
Result: PASS | FAIL ({N} issues found) ```
If ALL checks pass → Result: PASS If ANY check fails → Result: FAIL with count of failures
